data.title: Retail Traders Dump Single Stocks for First Time Since 2023
data.description:
Retail traders showed signs of capitulation on Monday, selling $20.6 million of shares. This marked the first day of net single-stock selling since November 2023, according to Vanda Research.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Retail demand has edged lower since the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28, with the S&P 500 (SPX) falling by 4% since then. "The trend since the start of March has been one of gradually receding retail participation, alongside systematic deleveraging and only modest buying from long-only and hedge fund investors on the other side," said Vanda Macro Strategist Ruta Prieskienyte.

Retail Traders Pick Up $262 Million of Shares after Brief Selling Spree

Retail traders have emerged as reliable buyers of downside price action since the bull market began three years ago. A break in the trend could create issues for the benchmark index as traders assess the impact of the U.S.-Iran war.

However, the selling didn't last long, as retail traders purchased $262.3 million worth of shares on Tuesday from the market open to 12:50 p.m. Eastern Time. The group purchased a record amount of shares last year, exceeding the previous record set in 2021 by 17%.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Regional Bank ETFs Under Pressure as Ohio CD Rates Hit 4% and Investors Flee to Gold
data.description:
Ohio certificate of deposit (CD) rates, which are fixed-interest savings products offered by banks, have climbed above 4% APY. As a result, one of the state's regional banks, LCNB Corporation (LCNB), is struggling to retain deposits despite offering higher rates.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This same pressure is now extending to regional bank ETFs, including the iShares U.S. Regional Banks ETF (IAT) and the SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF (KRE). Rising funding costs and geopolitical tensions appear to have rattled investors, prompting them to flee to safe-haven assets like gold.

Gold Gains as Regional Bank ETFs Face Pressure

Rising CD rates force banks to pay higher interest to keep deposits, increasing their funding costs and reducing profits. Because regional bank ETFs like IAT and KRE hold shares of these banks, weaker earnings can drag down their performance and prompt investors to pull back.

Geopolitical tensions in early March added to pressure on regional bank ETFs, with KRE and IAT down 5.80% and 6.80%, respectively, over the past month. This follows the U.S.-Israeli strike on Iran in late February, which triggered an oil price spike and renewed inflation fears. Investors grew more wary of prolonged high interest rates, deepening their concerns about regional bank stability, which in turn weighed on both KRE and IAT ETFs.

Historical stress in regional bank ETFs has often driven investors toward safe-haven assets. Gold hit an all-time high in late January 2026 as U.S.-China trade tensions and ensuing geopolitical developments bolstered investor demand. Meanwhile, central banks have been buying gold at a record pace for several years, and JPMorgan (JPM) projects continued strong demand in 2026, adding pressure on U.S. regional bank ETFs as investors seek safer assets.

Top Holdings Boost Regional Bank ETFs' Outlook

Despite pressure on regional bank ETFs, several individual holdings within the SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF and the iShares U.S. Regional Banks ETF have shown strong upside potential, according to TipRanks forecast data.

For KRE:

  • Banc of California Inc. (BANC): Analysts rate this stock a Strong Buy, with a projected average price target of $24.40 and an implied upside of 39.5%
  • United Community Banks Inc. (UCB): Analysts rate this stock a Moderate Buy, projecting an average price target of $115.39, with an implied upside of 269.5%
  • Coastal Financial Corp. (CCB): Analysts also rate this stock a Moderate Buy, with an average price target of $135.00 and an implied upside of 75.3%.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: "Million-Mile Battery": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Jumps With New Semi Battery Claims
data.description:
We have heard previously about electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) and its Semi, which is winning a lot of positive feedback from truckers. It turns out there is one more thing to like about the Semi, specifically, the sheer longevity of its battery. While it requires regular recharging, the battery itself will last for quite a long time: around one million miles. That was a big step forward, and investors piled in, sending Tesla shares up nearly 2% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade TSLA with leverage
  • New trading tool for TSLA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

Tesla Semi program lead Dan Priestley recently walked former Tonight Show host and car buff Jay Leno through the Semi operation, showing off the vehicle and detailing some of the engineering features. One of the big ones was a hefty weight reduction: 1,000 pounds of weight savings, which in turn allowed the 500-mile Semi to have a payload capacity that was roughly a match for diesel rigs.

Several other features jumped out, including 60% charging in 30 minutes, a steering system based on the Cybertruck, and more. But the clear winner was the million-mile battery, a battery pack built low into the chassis that is specifically designed to clear that threshold. Given that fleet operations tend to plan for vehicles with half a million to a million miles of service, the Semi becomes a viable part of such operations.

Another Chinese Competitor

Tesla has had to face down several Chinese competitors, between Tesla pushing into China and seeing Chinese competitors crop up in other markets. But the Semi already has one competitor looking for a piece of the market: Windrose Tech.

Windrose is not being shy about offering details on its version of the Semi. It will gladly reveal pricing information, range, weight, payload, and so on. It even will discuss why the Windrose version of the Semi looks a lot like the Semi itself. It turns out the answer is simple: "…that's what the aerodynamics dictate." The potential impact of the Windrose truck on the market is unclear, but there is some potential risk to Tesla involved.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 40.79% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $399.33 per share implies 2.51% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 3/25/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Wednesday amid falling oil prices and new developments in the U.S.-Iran war.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Both sides have outlined proposals to end the war, though key differences still remain. A senior Iranian official noted that the country's first reaction to the terms was "not positive," while a top U.S. official characterized Iran's demands as "ridiculous and unrealistic." Still, investors view these actions as a first step in securing a ceasefire.

Iran Sets Ceasefire Conditions, Vows Strikes Until They are Met

Iran laid out five key conditions to end the conflict and said that it would continue missile and drone strikes until they are met. These include war reparations, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and a full stop to "aggression and assassinations" by the U.S. and Israel.

Meanwhile, senior Iranian officials denied President Trump's claim of direct peace talks between the two sides and accused him of manipulating the oil and stock markets. Earlier this week, Trump called off an attack on the country's power plants for five days to allow for negotiations.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Globalstar Stock (GSAT) Surges 16% as Satellite Maker Benefits from SpaceX IPO Fever
data.description:
Shares in Globalstar (GSAT) rocketed higher today as investors hitched a ride on satellite and space infrastructure hopes such as the ever-looming SpaceX IPO.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Satellite Infrastructure

Globalstar, which describes itself as a next-generation telecommunications provider delivering reliable, next-generation connectivity solutions through both satellite and terrestrial infrastructure, was up 16% in mid-trading.

The company is known for powering the emergency SOS feature in newer Apple (AAPL) iPhones. Globalstar operates a network of low-earth orbit satellites that provide voice and data communications services in remote areas where traditional cellular networks don't reach.

Investors are getting excited about the potential of space commercialisation on reports that Elon Musk's Space X is preparing what could be a world-record high IPO. It is reportedly weighing up a ballpark figure of about $75 billion in the move which could take place in the next few days.

Traders therefore see the company as a key beneficiary of rising enthusiasm for the broader space economy.

Revenues on the Rise

But it is not just a vacuum of expectation. Back in February, Globalstar reported full year revenue of $273.0 million, representing a 9% increase from 2024.

This included a successful proof of concept with Parsons, continued investment in XCOM RAN-based 5G research to evaluate high-capacity private wireless architectures for defense applications, and selection as the technology partner by Virewirx for a Phase II Small Business Innovation Research contract with the Office of the Under Secretary of War.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Climbs as U.S.-Iran Outline Ceasefire Terms; Trump Forms Tech Advisory Council
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Wednesday after the U.S. and Iran put forward proposals to end the war.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

With Pakistan acting as an intermediary, the U.S. sent Iran a 15‑point plan to end the war, including ending uranium enrichment, pledging to never pursue nuclear weapons, and reopening the Strait of Hormuz as a free maritime corridor. Iran rejected the proposal and outlined its own list of conditions, such as war reparations, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and a binding guarantee that the U.S. and Israel won't declare war on it again.

"Someone like us will never get along with someone like you," said IRGC spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari. "Not now, not ever." Meanwhile, a top U.S. official labeled Iran's demands as "ridiculous and unrealistic." Still, investors viewed the exchange of terms as a first step toward a potential ceasefire.

Elsewhere, BlackRock (BLK) CEO Larry Fink cautioned that oil rising to $150 could trigger a "stark and steep recession." Brent crude futures (BZ), the international benchmark for oil, have climbed 26% to $97 since the U.S. and Israel first attacked Iran on February 28.

Fink said it's too early to predict the outcome of the war, though he anticipates one of two scenarios. In the first, Iran would be accepted back into the international community after the conflict, sending oil prices below their pre‑war levels. If the first scenario fails, the second is far less optimistic, with Fink warning of a recession and " years of above $100, closer to $150 oil, which has profound implications in the economy."

Finally, President Trump has commissioned the creation of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST), which will advise the administration on advances in AI, quantum computing, biotechnology, and other emerging technologies. The council will have 24 members including the CEOs of Nvidia (NVDA), Meta Platforms (META), Google (GOOGL), and Dell (DELL), according to the Wall Street Journal.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.84% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Corcept Rockets 48% after FDA Lifyorli Approval — What Does This Mean for CORT Stock?
data.description:
Shares in biopharma company Corcept Therapeutics climbed as high as 48% to $48.35 following U.S. approval of Lifyorli for treating certain ovarian-type cancers. H.C. Wainwright believes the move could enable the California-based company to finally diversify beyond Korlym.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Both Lifyorli (relacorilant) and Korlym (mifepristone) are used for patients with Cushing's syndrome, a condition where the body makes too much of the stress hormone, cortisol. However, while Korlym aims to fight high blood sugar caused by that excess cortisol, Lifyorli is designed to block the cortisol's harmful effects more selectively.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Lifyorli for use in combination with chemotherapy drug nab-paclitaxel to treat adults whose ovarian-type cancer has stopped responding to standard platinum-based chemotherapy. Such patients are expected to have undergone between one and three prior therapies, including bevacizumab, before qualifying to use the newly approved combination.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Oil at $150 Will Trigger a 'Steep Recession,' Warns BlackRock CEO
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) CEO and co-founder Larry Fink has warned that oil rising to $150 could lead to a "stark and steep recession." Brent crude futures (BZ), the international benchmark for oil, have surged 26% to $96 since the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Fink noted that it is too early to determine the outcome of the war, although he expects one to two scenarios to unfold. The first is for Iran to be integrated into the international community following the end of the war, which would send oil below its pre-war level.

Strait of Hormuz Tensions Keep Oil Elevated

If the first scenario doesn't play out, the second scenario is much less rosy, with Fink calling for a recession and "years of above $100, closer to $150 oil, which has profound implications in the economy."

While traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has slightly picked up in recent days, it's still well below the rate seen before the start of the war. Iran has continued to reiterate that it will attack any U.S.-linked vessel crossing through the passageway and rejected an initial U.S. proposal to end the war, citing the demands as "excessive."
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: CD Projekt Stock (OTGLY) Jumps Despite AI Game Development Integration
data.description:
CD Projekt (OTGLY) stock was up on Wednesday alongside news that the video game publisher has started implementing AI into its game development process. A new management report from the company noted that CD Projekt Red has started to prototype and test AI solutions for The Witcher 4 and Cyberpunk 2.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The report from CD Projekt claimed that this AI technology is being used to create more realistic non-player characters (NPCs). Specifically, it's dedicated to adjusting the movement of large crowds of NPCs to make them appear more realistic to players. CD Projekt also said that this is one of roughly a dozen projects that its AI team is working on.

While CD Projekt stock is up today, the company could face backlash over its decision to include AI in the development of its games. Generative AI has received pushback from gamers, who don't want developers to replace human artistic visions with those generated by AI. This has resulted in terms like "AI slop" being applied to games that use the technology.

CD Projekt Stock Movement Today

CD Projekt stock was up 1.32% on Wednesday but was still down 3.57% year-to-date. The stock has also risen 17.26% over the past 12 months.

OTGLY stock movement today saw some 1,090 shares change hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 1,240 shares.
data.author: William White
